title: Recover the Modified Max Heap After Deletion

statement.markdown: Given a sorted array representing a max heap and a list of indices of elements to be deleted from the max heap, restore the max heap by performing the necessary deletion operations. The deletion operation should be done in-place without using any extra space.
statement.inputFormat: The first argument `nums` is a sorted array representing a max heap. The second argument `indToDelete` is a list of indices of elements to be deleted from the max heap.
statement.outputFormat: Restore the modified max heap by returning a boolean indicating whether the max heap property is maintained after deletion.
statement.notes: The deletion operation should be performed in-place and the max heap property must be maintained after deletion.

constraints.description: Size of list of indices to delete

examples.explanation: The max heap is restored after deleting the elements at indices 1 and 2.

hints.text: Use a recursive approach to delete elements from the max heap

editorial.title: Recover the Modified Max Heap After Deletion
editorial.intuition: To restore the max heap, we need to delete the elements at the given indices in a way that maintains the max heap property.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to create a new max heap by iterating over the array and deleting the elements at the given indices, which would require O(n) time complexity and O(n) space complexity.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use a recursive approach to delete the elements from the max heap, which would require O(n + m) time complexity and O(log n) space complexity.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution would be to use the recursive approach to delete the elements from the max heap while maintaining the max heap property, which would require O(n + m) time complexity and O(log n) space complexity.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proved by showing that the max heap property is maintained after deletion.
editorial.dryRun: Perform a dry run of the solution to understand how it works.
commonMistakes: Not maintaining the max heap property after deletion
Using an inefficient approach to delete elements

starterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean recoverMaxHeap(int[] nums, int[] indToDelete); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def recoverMaxHeap(self, nums: list[int], indToDelete: list[int]) -> bool: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool recoverMaxHeap(std::vector<int>& nums, const std::vector<int>& indToDelete) { int i = 0; for (int idx : indToDelete) { nums[idx] = INT_MIN; } for (i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++) { if (nums[i] == INT_MIN) { continue; } int left = 2 * i + 1; int right = 2 * i + 2; if (left >= nums.size()) { continue; } if (right >= nums.size()) { maxHeapify(nums, i, left); continue; } if (nums[i] < nums[left] || nums[i] < nums[right]) { maxHeapify(nums, i, left); maxHeapify(nums, i, right); } } return true; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean recoverMaxHeap(int[] nums, int[] indToDelete) { int i = 0; for (int idx : indToDelete) { nums[idx] = Integer.MIN_VALUE; } for (i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { if (nums[i] == Integer.MIN_VALUE) { continue; } int left = 2 * i + 1; int right = 2 * i + 2; if (left >= nums.length) { continue; } if (right >= nums.length) { maxHeapify(nums, i, left); continue; } if (nums[i] < nums[left] || nums[i] < nums[right]) { maxHeapify(nums, i, left); maxHeapify(nums, i, right); } } return true; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def recoverMaxHeap(self, nums: list[int], indToDelete: list[int]) -> bool: for idx in indToDelete: nums[idx] = float('-inf') for i in range(len(nums)): if nums[i] == float('-inf'): continue left = 2 * i + 1 right = 2 * i + 2 if left >= len(nums): continue if right >= len(nums): self.maxHeapify(nums, i, left); continue if nums[i] < nums[left] or nums[i] < nums[right]: self.maxHeapify(nums, i, left); self.maxHeapify(nums, i, right); return True
buggyStarterCode.javascript: var recoverMaxHeap = function(nums, indToDelete) { for (var idx of indToDelete) { nums[idx] = -Infinity; } for (var i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { if (nums[i] === -Infinity) { continue; } var left = 2 * i + 1; var right = 2 * i + 2; if (left >= nums.length) { continue; } if (right >= nums.length) { this.maxHeapify(nums, i, left); continue; } if (nums[i] < nums[left] || nums[i] < nums[right]) { this.maxHeapify(nums, i, left); this.maxHeapify(nums, i, right); } } return true; };

functionSignature.cpp: bool recoverMaxHeap(std::vector<int>& nums, const std::vector<int>& indToDelete);
functionSignature.python: def recoverMaxHeap(self, nums: list[int], indToDelete: list[int]) -> bool:

solutions.python:
class Solution: def recoverMaxHeap(self, nums: list[int], indToDelete: list[int]) -> bool: for idx in indToDelete: nums[idx] = float('-inf') n = len(nums) for i in range(n): if nums[i] == float('-inf'): continue left = 2 * i + 1 right = 2 * i + 2 if left >= n: continue if right >= n: self.maxHeapify(nums, i, left); continue if nums[i] < nums[left] or nums[i] < nums[right]: self.maxHeapify(nums, i, left); self.maxHeapify(nums, i, right); return True

def maxHeapify(self, nums, i, j): if nums[i] < nums[j]: nums[i], nums[j] = nums[j], nums[i] while i > 0 and nums[i] < nums[(i - 1) // 2]: nums[i], nums[(i - 1) // 2] = nums[(i - 1) // 2], nums[i] i = (i - 1) // 2
